optimal_view.narrative: **Is it always 12 stocks?** No magic number - it is a deliberate choice, not an accident. The pure-math 'optimal' portfolio (the red star) maximises historical risk-adjusted return with no guardrails, and here it collapses into just **7 names**, with **47% piled into its top two**. That looks great on paper because unconstrained optimisation chases whatever happened to do best in the past. Our recommended book instead spreads across **12 names** with a **15% cap** and a **5% floor** on each. With those guardrails the maths alone bounds you to roughly **7-20 holdings**, and we target ~12 as the sweet spot between real diversification and staying focused. The pay-off is in the **out-of-sample** test (money the model never saw): the regularized book delivered **18.7%** vs the concentrated optimum's **-0.3%** - so spreading out cost us nothing real and bought us safety. Bottom line: we show you the textbook optimum for transparency, but we recommend the **robust** version - the one whose track record holds up on data it had never seen.

selection_method: PEG-based stock selection for maximum CAGR optimization
notes: v1 MVP: forecast layer uses precomputed forecast columns; backtest/walk-forward use yfinance adjusted prices.
